steps.title: Recognize exponential decay eliminates most choices
steps.content:
The scatterplot shows a decreasing curve: as $x$ increases, $y$ decreases.

This pattern indicates exponential decay, which requires $0 < b < 1$ (the base must be less than 1).

Choices B ($1.83$), C ($18.36$), and D ($126.35$) are all greater than 1, representing exponential growth, which contradicts the scatterplot.

Only choice A ($0.83$) satisfies $0 < b < 1$.

steps.title: Calculate $b$ from two points on the curve
steps.content:
Identify two clear points from the scatterplot: $(1, 110)$ and $(9, 18)$. For the exponential model $y = a(b)^x$, the ratio of output values equals $b$ raised to the difference in input values:
$$\frac{y_2}{y_1} = b^{x_2 - x_1}$$
Substituting:
$$\frac{18}{110} = b^{9-1}$$
$$\frac{9}{55} = b^8$$
$$b = \left(\frac{9}{55}\right)^{1/8} \approx 0.80$$
Among the choices, $0.83$ is closest to this calculated value. That matches choice A — A is correct.
